Identity.History: Khosrow II ruled the Sasanian Empire from 590–628 AD, with a brief interruption early in his reign. Restored to power with Byzantine help, he later waged a massive war against Byzantium, conquering Syria, Egypt, and much of Anatolia. His reign reached the greatest territorial extent of the Sasanian Empire, but the long war exhausted Persia. Emperor Heraclius counterattacked, and Khosrow was overthrown and executed by his son Kavadh II. His fall triggered civil war and rapid Sasanian decline before the Arab conquests.
